name: Microwave Veggie Rice Bowl
instructions: Microwave the rice according to package directions.
Heat the frozen vegetables and black beans in a microwave-safe bowl.
Scramble or poach the egg in the microwave.
Combine everything and season with soy sauce.

name: No-Cook Hummus Wrap
instructions: Spread hummus over the tortilla.
Add spinach, cucumber, and shredded carrot.
Roll tightly and slice in half.

name: Dorm Chicken Quesadilla
instructions: Place chicken, cheese, and salsa on half of the tortilla.
Fold the tortilla and heat in a pan or microwave until the cheese melts.
Slice and serve.

name: Peanut Butter Banana Oats
instructions: Mix oats and milk in a bowl.
Microwave until soft.
Top with banana slices, peanut butter, and honey.

name: Gluten-Free Tuna Potato Bowl
instructions: Microwave the potato until tender.
Mix tuna with greek yogurt, chopped green onion, and salt.
Split the potato and spoon the tuna mixture on top.

name: Quick Tofu Noodles
instructions: Cook the noodles according to package directions.
Warm tofu and vegetables.
Stir soy sauce and peanut butter into the noodles, then top with tofu and vegetables.

name: Yogurt Berry Parfait
instructions: Layer yogurt, berries, and granola in a bowl or cup.
Drizzle honey on top.

name: Chickpea Salad Bowl
instructions: Drain and rinse the chickpeas.
Dice the vegetables and add them to a bowl.
Dress with olive oil and lemon juice, then toss.

name: Kettle Couscous Chickpea Cup
instructions: Pour boiling water from the kettle over couscous in a heat-safe bowl.
Cover until fluffy.
Stir in chickpeas, peas, olive oil, and lemon pepper.

name: Mini Fridge Protein Snack Box
instructions: Slice the hard boiled eggs.
Pack eggs, cheese, grapes, carrots, and hummus into a plate or container.
Season the eggs with salt or pepper if available.

name: Toaster Avocado Egg Toast
instructions: Toast the bread.
Microwave or soft scramble the egg.
Mash avocado on toast, add the egg, and finish with hot sauce and salt.

name: Blender Berry Protein Smoothie
instructions: Add milk, protein powder, berries, banana, and ice to a blender.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into a cup and drink cold.

name: Dairy-Free Rice Cooker Lentil Pilaf
instructions: Rinse rice and lentils.
Add rice, lentils, broth, vegetables, and cumin to the rice cooker.
Cook until tender, then fluff and serve.

name: Air Fryer Salmon Sweet Potato Plate
instructions: Cube the sweet potato and toss with olive oil.
Air fry sweet potato until nearly tender.
Add salmon and broccoli, then cook until the salmon flakes.
Finish with lemon.

name: Low-Cost Bean and Cheese Tostadas
instructions: Warm refried beans in the microwave.
Spread beans over tostada shells.
Top with cheese, salsa, and lettuce.

name: No-Cook Tuna Cracker Stack
instructions: Mix tuna with relish, mayo, and pepper.
Spoon the tuna mixture onto crackers.
Serve immediately for a crunchy snack.

name: Kettle Ramen Egg Bowl
instructions: Use kettle water to soften the ramen noodles.
Microwave an egg until set.
Add spinach, soy sauce, green onion, and the egg to the noodles.

name: Vegetarian Sheetless Meal Prep Burrito Bowls
instructions: Cook rice in the rice cooker.
Warm beans and corn in the microwave.
Portion rice, beans, corn, salsa, cheese, and avocado into containers.
Refrigerate extra portions for later.

name: 30-Minute Tomato Soup Grilled Cheese
instructions: Warm tomato soup in the microwave.
Butter bread and add cheese.
Toast the sandwich in a pan until golden.
Serve with soup and black pepper.

name: Stocked Dorm Stir-Fry Night
instructions: Cook rice in the rice cooker.
Sear tofu in a pan on the hot plate.
Add broccoli and edamame until hot.
Toss with soy sauce and sesame oil, then serve over rice.

name: Dairy-Free Turkey Rice Cooker Chili
instructions: Add turkey, beans, tomatoes, corn, chili powder, and rice to the rice cooker.
Cook until the rice is tender and turkey is fully cooked.
Stir well and portion leftovers into containers.

name: Nut-Free Overnight Oats
instructions: Stir oats, milk, berries, honey, and chia seeds in a container.
Refrigerate until thickened.
Eat cold or warm briefly in the microwave.

name: Dairy-Free Peanut Noodle Salad
instructions: Soften noodles with kettle water and drain.
Stir peanut butter and soy sauce into a quick sauce.
Toss noodles with cucumber, carrot, and sauce.

name: Apple Cinnamon Yogurt Bowl
instructions: Dice the apple into small pieces.
Spoon yogurt into a bowl.
Top with apple, cinnamon, honey, and granola.

name: Microwave Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Warm rice and frozen vegetables in a microwave-safe bowl.
Stir in the egg and microwave in short bursts until set.
Season with soy sauce and green onion.

name: Air Fryer Chickpea Tacos
instructions: Toss chickpeas with taco seasoning.
Air fry until crisp.
Fill tortillas with chickpeas, lettuce, and salsa.

name: Rice Cooker Coconut Curry
instructions: Add rice, coconut milk, chickpeas, curry powder, and vegetables to the rice cooker.
Cook until rice is tender.
Stir and rest for five minutes before serving.

name: Toaster Pizza Bagel
instructions: Toast the bagel halves lightly.
Add pizza sauce, mozzarella, bell pepper, and oregano.
Microwave until the cheese melts.

name: Kettle Miso Tofu Soup
instructions: Pour hot kettle water over miso paste and stir.
Add tofu, noodles, spinach, and green onion.
Cover until noodles soften.

name: Mini Fridge Caprese Snack Plate
instructions: Slice mozzarella and tomato.
Arrange with basil and crackers.
Drizzle olive oil over the plate.

name: Blender Mango Lassi Smoothie
instructions: Add mango, yogurt, milk, honey, and cardamom to the blender.
Blend until creamy.
Serve cold.

name: Air Fryer Turkey Meatball Bowl
instructions: Air fry turkey meatballs until hot.
Microwave rice and broccoli.
Top with marinara and parmesan.

name: No-Cook Black Bean Salsa Cups
instructions: Drain black beans and corn.
Mix with salsa and diced avocado.
Scoop with tortilla chips.

name: Hot Plate Garlic Shrimp Noodles
instructions: Cook noodles and set aside.
Saute shrimp, garlic, and vegetables in a pan.
Toss with noodles and soy sauce.

name: Rice Cooker Breakfast Quinoa
instructions: Add quinoa, milk, cinnamon, and chia seeds to the rice cooker.
Cook until quinoa is tender.
Top with sliced banana.

name: Microwave Loaded Sweet Potato
instructions: Pierce the sweet potato and microwave until tender.
Warm black beans in a small microwave-safe bowl.
Split the potato and top with beans, greek yogurt, salsa, and green onion.
notes: Uses one bowl and turns pantry beans into a filling meal.

name: No-Cook Mediterranean Snack Board
instructions: Slice pita and cucumber.
Arrange hummus, olives, feta, pita, and cucumber on a plate.
Eat as a snack board or fold into mini pita pockets.
notes: A dorm-friendly no-cook plate for nights when the dining hall is closed.

name: Kettle Apple Cinnamon Oat Cup
instructions: Add oats, diced apple, cinnamon, and raisins to a heat-safe cup.
Pour in hot kettle water and cover for five minutes.
Stir in maple syrup before eating.
notes: Works with shelf-stable breakfast staples and no fridge space.

name: Toaster Turkey Pesto Melt
instructions: Toast the bread until sturdy.
Layer turkey, mozzarella, pesto, and tomato.
Microwave briefly until the cheese softens.
notes: Uses toaster texture plus a short microwave melt instead of an oven.

name: Hot Plate Shakshuka for One
instructions: Cook onion and bell pepper in a pan on the hot plate.
Add tomato sauce and cumin, then simmer until thick.
Crack in eggs, cover, and cook until the whites set.
notes: A one-pan hot plate dinner with leftovers for toast or rice.

name: Rice Cooker Chicken Biryani Bowl
instructions: Rinse rice and add it to the rice cooker with water and biryani spice.
Stir in cooked chicken and frozen peas.
Cook until rice is fluffy, then top with fried onions.
notes: Uses pre-cooked chicken so it stays safe and realistic for dorm kitchens.

name: Air Fryer Tofu Nuggets and Fries
instructions: Press tofu briefly with paper towels and cut into cubes.
Toss tofu with soy sauce and cornstarch.
Cut potatoes into wedges and air fry with tofu until crisp.
Serve with ketchup.
notes: Air fryer crispness without needing a shared kitchen oven.

name: Blender Green Smoothie Bowl
instructions: Blend spinach, banana, frozen mango, and oat milk until thick.
Pour into a bowl.
Top with granola.
notes: A quick produce-heavy meal when a blender is the only appliance.

name: Microwave Mac and Peas
instructions: Microwave macaroni with water in a deep bowl until tender.
Drain carefully, then stir in cheese sauce, milk, and peas.
Microwave once more and finish with black pepper.
notes: A cheap comfort meal with vegetables added.

name: Pan Turkey Taco Skillet
instructions: Brown turkey in a pan on the hot plate.
Add beans, corn, taco seasoning, and a splash of water.
Serve over warm rice.
notes: A high-protein skillet that can be portioned into two meals.

name: Rice Cooker Red Beans and Rice
instructions: Add rice, beans, celery, bell pepper, seasoning, and water to the rice cooker.
Cook until the rice is tender and vegetables are soft.
Let it sit on warm for ten minutes before serving.
notes: A longer hands-off meal for meal prep days.

name: Air Fryer Steak Fajita Bowl
instructions: Toss steak strips, bell pepper, and onion with seasoning.
Air fry until steak is cooked through.
Microwave rice and top with fajita mix and lime.
notes: A higher-budget dorm dinner that still avoids a full kitchen.

name: Big Grocery Trip Sushi Bowl
instructions: Cook sushi rice in the rice cooker and let it cool slightly.
Slice cucumber, avocado, and cooked or smoked salmon.
Layer rice, salmon, vegetables, nori, and soy sauce in a bowl.
notes: Best after a grocery run when the dorm fridge is stocked.

name: Dorm Charcuterie Dinner Box
instructions: Slice cheddar if needed.
Arrange turkey, cheddar, crackers, grapes, and pickles in a box or plate.
Pack extra crackers separately so they stay crisp.
notes: A no-cook higher-budget option for grazing through a study session.

name: Celebration Air Fryer Chicken Plate
instructions: Cook rice in the rice cooker.
Toss chicken thighs, potatoes, and green beans with olive oil and lemon.
Air fry in batches until chicken is cooked through and potatoes are tender.
Serve over rice with extra lemon.
notes: A splurge meal for sharing when a student has more groceries and time.
